project.description:
The Studio Workspace — a reusable, spec-driven workspace for browsing a studio's catalogues inside the V2 workspace shell. Composes existing substrate pieces with zero new chrome JS:
- TreeWidget — a WorkspaceWidget that fetches a catalogue subtree as canonical TreeNode JSON and renders it with the generic TreeRenderer (homing-core-js). - CatalogueTreeGetAction — GET /catalogue-tree?id=<slug> — serializes a Catalogue subtree via CatalogueTreeAdapter + TreeNodeJsonWriter. Wired downstream via Fixtures.harnessGetActions() with the studio's root catalogue. - StudioWorkspaceSpec — a WorkspaceSpec (kind "studio") that pins the TreeWidget; mounted by GenericWorkspace at ?app=genericWorkspace&ws_kind=studio.
First widget is the tree view; a studio-navigation Party (tree selection -> open doc in a tab) lands in a follow-up.
Doctrinal anchors: A Good Design Doesn't Pick Up Its Substrate (the spec declares only what's studio-specific), Make It Impossible Not Forbidden (no hand-written chrome JS — only the declarative surface).
